2606.14600.title: LoSoNA: A Benchmark for Local Social Norm Adaptation in Group Conversations
2606.14600.paper_summary: Online group chats are social spaces with local conversational norms that are rarely stated explicitly. The ability and willingness of LLM-based agents to recognize and adapt to these norms remains mostly unexplored. We introduce LoSoNA, a benchmark for local social norm adaptation in multi-party chat. Each scenario gives a subject model a curated group-chat transcript in which non-subject participants demonstrate a hidden local norm, followed by a final elicitor turn that forces a response revealing whether the subject has inferred that norm. We evaluate eight frontier and open-weight models under four prompting conditions that vary how explicitly the model is told to treat the prior conversation as evidence for how it should answer. Naive prompting remains limited for most models; explicit norm-aware prompting helps unevenly, with Gemini 3.1 Pro reaching $84.2\%$ and Claude Fable 5 reaching $81.6\%$, while several other models show small gains or regressions. LoSoNA contributes to recent calls for evaluating LLM social capabilities by testing whether models can infer local conversational norms from precedent and use them in a one-turn group-chat response.
2606.14600.paper_summary_zh: 線上群組聊天是具有地方性對話規範的社交空間，這些規範很少被明確陳述。基於LLM的代理人識別和適應這些規範的能力和意願仍然大多未被探索。我們介紹LoSoNA，一個用於多方聊天中地方社會規範適應的基準。每個場景都給一個主體模型提供一個精心策劃的群組聊天記錄，其中非主體參與者展示了一個隱藏的地方規範，隨後是一個最終的引導回合，迫使回應揭示主體是否推斷出該規範。我們在四種提示條件下評估了八個前沿和開放權重模型，這些條件變化了模型被告知如何將之前的對話視為回答的證據的明確程度。對於大多數模型而言，天真的提示仍然有限；明確的規範意識提示幫助不均，Gemini 3.1 Pro達到$84.2\%$，而Claude Fable 5達到$81.6\%$，同時其他幾個模型顯示出小幅增長或退步。LoSoNA對於最近呼籲評估LLM社會能力的研究作出貢獻，通過測試模型是否能從先例中推斷地方對話規範並在一次回合的群組聊天回應中使用它們。
2606.14600.author: Mateusz Winiarek et.al.
2606.14600.authors: Mateusz Winiarek, Maksymilian Bilski, Mateusz Jacniacki
